Name: Bellossom
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Bellossom is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Bellossom is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Bellossom gather at times and seem to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.

Name: Espeon
Effects: Move up to 4 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy’s actions.

Name: Forretress
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Does 30 damage times the number of Pineco and Forretress you have in play. This attack does 30 damage to each of your Pineco and Forretress in play.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: If, before Gliscor does damage, the Defending Pokémon has no damage counters on it and is then damaged by this attack (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: If your opponent has any [F] Pokémon in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage.
Your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
You may discard any number of [R] Energy cards attached to Magcargo. If you do , this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage for each [R] Energy card you discarded.
Flavor-Text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

Name: Scizor
Flavor-Text: Its wings are not used for flying. They are flapped at high speed to adjust its body temperature.

Name: Smeargle
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Smeargle is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent’s hand. If you do, choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. This power can’t be used if Smeargle is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

Name: Togekiss
Effects: Remove all damage counters from each of your Pokémon. Shuffle Togekiss and all cards attached to it back into your deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: As everyone knows, it visits peaceful regions, bringing them gifts of kindness and sweet blessings.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Umbreon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 damage.
Flavor-Text: When agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.

Name: Dodrio
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Dodrio is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] less.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It collects data and plans three times as wisely, but it may think too much and fall into a state of immobility.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Drifblim does 20 damage to itself.

Shuffle Drifblim and all cards attached to it back into your deck. Then, your opponent shuffles the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck. (You choose your new Active Pokémon first.)
Flavor-Text: It can generate and release gas within its body. That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

Name: Forretress
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may switch Forretress with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It remains immovably rooted to its tree. It scatters pieces of its hard shell to drive its enemies away.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: You may do 80 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Hariyama does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each [D] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Does 50 damage damage times the number of Special Conditions affecting the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

Name: Metagross
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It folds its four legs when flying. Its four brains are said to be superior to a supercomputer.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Look at your opponent’s hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

Name: Rotom
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your face-down Prize cards with the top card of your deck. This power can’t be used if Rotom is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Search your deck for a [M] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Remove 6 damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It’s a highly favored tropical snack.

Name: Vespiquen
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched [G] Pokémon by attacks.
Effects: During your next turn, Vespiquen’s Retreat Cost is 0.
Flavor-Text: It raises grubs in the holes in its body. It secretes pheromones to control Combee.

Name: Vileplume
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each player can’t play any Trainer cards from his or her hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

Name: Weavile
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Weavile from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at your opponent’s hand. Choose a card from your opponent’s hand and discard it.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in snowy regions. It carves patterns in trees with its claws as a signal to others.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Flareon.
Flavor-Text: It stores some of the air it inhales in its internal flame pouch, which heats it to over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Gloom
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Special Condition. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Jolteon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a [M] Energy card and attach it to Lairon.
Flavor-Text: It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When two Beldum fuse together, a magnetic nervous system places their brains in union.

Name: Muk
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of a powerful poison. Touching it accidentally will cause a fever that requires bed rest.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With its pincer horns, it digs burrows to sleep in at night. In the morning, damp soil clings to its body.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its whiskers help it to maintain balance. Its fangs never stop growing, so it gnaws to pare them down.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Search your discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.
If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Scyther
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Scyther would be damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that attack’s damage done to Scyther.
Flavor-Text: When it moves, it leaves only a blur. If it hides in grass, its protective coloration makes it invisible.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It attacks by spraying a repugnant fluid from its tail, but the stench dulls after a few squirts.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions and 3 damage counters from Slowbro.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, Slowbro is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Naturally dull to begin with, it lost its ability to feel pain due to Shellder’s seeping poison.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage done to Togetic during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It grows dispirited if it is not with kind people. It can float in midair without moving its wings.

Name: Unown
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a [D] Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Vaporeon.
Choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When Vaporeon’s fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

Name: Aron
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: When it evolves, it sheds the steel carapace that covered its whole body and develops a new one.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground’s natural magnetism, letting it float.

Name: Combee
Effects: If Vespiquen is on your Bench and has any damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a thousand, packed closely together in a lump.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: By alternately raising and lowering its two heads, it balances itself to be more stable while running.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Drifloon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its irregularly configured DNA is affected by its surroundings. It evolves if its environment changes.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

Name: Grimer
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Wherever Grimer has passed, so many germs are left behind that no plants will ever grow again.

Name: Hitmonchan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attack, including damage, done to Hitmonchan during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

Name: Hitmonlee
Flavor-Text: If it starts kicking repeatedly, both legs will stretch even longer to strike a fleeing foe.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

Name: Houndour
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To corner prey, they check each other’s location using barks that only they can understand.

Name: Makuhita
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put it into your hand. If not, discard it and draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Each player draws 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: It loves to bite and yank people’s hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Murkrow
Flavor-Text: It is said that when chased, it lures its attacker onto dark mountain trails where the foe will get lost.

Name: Oddish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Awakened by moonlight, it roams actively at night. In the day, it stays quietly underground.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon roasts hard berries with electricity to make them tender enough to eat.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It hangs and waits for flying insect prey to come near. It does not move about much on its own.

Name: Pineco
Effects: During your next turn, Pineco’s Surprise Attack attack’s base damage is 80.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.

Name: Rattata
Flavor-Text: Living wherever there is food available, it ceaselessly scavenges for edibles the entire day.

Name: Scyther
Effects: During your next turn, Scyther can’t use Slashing Strike.
Flavor-Text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose as many Basic Pokémon as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: A sweet sap leaks from its tail’s tip. Although not nutritious, the tail is pleasant to chew on.

Name: Slugma
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a [R] Energy card, attach it to Slugma. This power can’t be used if Slugma is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Vicious in nature, it drives Pidgey from their nests and scavenges any leftovers it can find.

Name: Stunky
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The foul fluid from its rear is so revolting that it can make people feel queasy up to a mile and a quarter away.

Name: Togepi
Effects: Ask your opponent if you may draw 2 cards. If yes, draw 2 cards. If no, this attack does 20 damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A proverb claims that happiness will come to anyone who can make a sleeping Togepi stand up.

Name: Burned Tower
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, the player searches his or her discard pile for a basic Energy card, shows it to his or her opponent, and put it into his or her hand.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Defender
Description: Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your opponent’s next turn. Any damage done to the Pokémon Defender is attached to by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Energy Exchanger
Description: Choose an Energy card from your hand, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Flower Shop Lady
Description: Search your discard pile for 3 Pokémon and 3 basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Legend Box
Description: Reveal the top 10 cards of your deck. If you reveal both halves of a Pokémon LEGEND, put those cards onto your Bench and attach all revealed Energy cards to that Pokémon LEGEND. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can play only 1 Pokémon LEGEND in this way.)

Name: Ruins of Alph
Description: Each Pokémon in play has no Resistance.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Sage’s Training
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose any 2 cards you find there and put them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Team Rocket’s Trickery
Description: Draw 2 cards. Then, your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Darkness Energy
Description: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn’t [D]. Darkness Energy provides [D] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Metal Energy
Description: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn’t [M] Metal Energy provides [M] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Espeon
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Espeon can use the attacks of all Pokémon you have in play that evolve from Eevee as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

Name: Houndoom
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. This power can’t be used if Houndoom is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Raichu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [L] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Raichu. This power can’t be used if Raichu is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to Raichu.

Name: Scizor
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all damage done to Scizor by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have any Special Energy cards attached to them.
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to Scizor.

Name: Slowking
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may reveal the top 2 cards of your deck and your opponent chooses 1 of them. Put that card into your hand and the other card on the bottom of your deck. This power can’t be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.

Name: Umbreon
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Umbreon is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, return Umbreon and all cards attached to it to your hand. This power can’t be used if Umbreon is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Pokémon in play that evolves from Eevee.

Name: Kyogre & Groudon LEGEND
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards from your opponent’s deck. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard the top 5 cards from your deck. This attack does 100 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Kyogre & Groudon LEGEND.

Name: Kyogre & Groudon LEGEND
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards from your opponent’s deck. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard the top 5 cards from your deck. This attack does 100 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Kyogre & Groudon LEGEND.

Name: Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy attached to Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND.

Name: Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy attached to Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Rayquaza & Deoxys LEGEND.

Name: Alph Lithograph
Description: RETURN ANY STADIUM CARD IN PLAY TO ITS PLAYERS HAND!
